title: Eragrostis blakei
taxonomy: Eragrostis blakei is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae. This classification further categorizes the plant into the tribe Eragrosteae and the genus Eragrostis. The species is also known as Blake's love grass, which provides a further distinction within the plant's classification. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.

intro: Eragrostis blakei, also known as Blake's love grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eragrostis blakei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
